tata.FY2023-24: AMBIGUOUS: FY2023 is consolidated while FY2024 is standalone

sections.about: This metric measures biomedical waste generated under BRSR Principle 6, Essential Indicator on category-wise waste generation. In steel companies, it generally relates to occupational health centres, hospitals, dispensaries and medical facilities rather than the core steelmaking process. A lower value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: Tata reported 256 MT in FY2025, substantially above JSW at 0.1958 MT, Jindal at 0.35 MT and SAIL at zero. Tata’s increase from 23 MT in FY2024 to 256 MT in FY2025 suggests either expanded healthcare boundary, disposal timing or operational medical-waste generation; the retrieved extracts do not provide a specific driver. SAIL and Jindal reported near-zero/zero values in FY2025, implying limited biomedical facilities or different disposal accounting.
sections.tataPositioning: Tata ranked 4th in FY2025. Because SAIL reported zero biomedical waste, a percentage gap from best is not meaningful; Tata’s absolute gap is 256 MT. Tata’s rank remained 4th despite FY2024 improvement because FY2025 worsened materially.
sections.comparabilityNotes: Biomedical waste depends heavily on medical-facility footprint and reporting boundary, not only steel production route. Tata Steel FY2022-23 data is reported on a consolidated basis rather than standalone.

flags.flagNote: Tata FY2023-to-FY2024 trend is ambiguous; specific FY2025 Tata driver was not identified in retrieved extracts.
